tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Cars are one of the most common personal transportation vehicles used worldwide. They are designed to carry passengers comfortably on roads and highways.

tab2.title: History
tab2.content: The first modern automobile was developed in the late 19th century by Karl Benz. Since then cars have evolved dramatically with improvements in safety, performance and technology.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Modern cars include electric vehicles, hybrid cars and self-driving technologies. Brands like Toyota, Tesla, BMW and Mercedes-Benz dominate the global market.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Motorcycles are two-wheeled motor vehicles designed for speed, agility and efficiency. They are widely used for commuting, racing and long-distance travel.

tab2.title: History
tab2.content: Motorcycles were developed in the late 1800s shortly after the invention of the automobile. Over time they evolved into multiple categories such as sport bikes, cruisers and touring bikes.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Some of the most famous motorcycle brands include Yamaha, Honda, Ducati, Kawasaki and Harley-Davidson.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Aircraft are vehicles designed to travel through the air. They include airplanes, helicopters, fighter jets and cargo aircraft.

tab2.title: History
tab2.content: The first successful powered flight was achieved by the Wright Brothers in 1903. Since then aviation technology has advanced rapidly.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Modern aircraft can travel faster than the speed of sound and carry hundreds of passengers across continents.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Ships are large water vessels designed for transporting goods and passengers across seas and oceans.

tab2.title: History
tab2.content: Ships have been used for thousands of years for exploration, trade and naval warfare.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Some ships such as aircraft carriers are among the largest and most powerful vehicles ever built.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Other vehicles include special transportation machines that do not fit into standard categories like cars or aircraft.

tab2.title: Usage
tab2.content: These vehicles are used for unique purposes such as rural transportation, snow travel or tourism.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Examples include bullock carts, horse carriages, rickshaws, snowmobiles and hoverboards.
